description: Having life; not dead or inanimate. 'Lima' names the fundamental quality of living beings—the spark that animates, the breath that continues, the presence that distinguishes the quick from the still.
sound_symbolism: The word breathes with life. 'Li' begins with liquid aliveness, life flowing. Then 'ma' grounds in embodiment, life made flesh.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'lima phireo' (alive plant). Fundamental to discussions of life, death, and vitality. Related to 'limoe' (creature) through shared root.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: All that is alive shares the quality of sentience—'lima' names this common ground of living beings.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional cultures recognized aliveness as sacred—the animating force that demands respect in all its forms.
